title: entandrophragma palustre
intro: Entandrophragma palustre, also known as the swamp teak, is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae, native to West and Central Africa. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Entandrophragma palustre include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
